newsSinceJun6: Record May 2026 revenue of NT$15.06B (+47.5% YoY, +7.3% MoM; Jan-May +27.4% YoY) reported June 8-9; on June 9 the stock exited TWSE disposition trading measures ('chu guan') and went straight to the +10% limit (826 TWD) with ~10,000 lots queued to buy; Citi reiterated Buy and raised its target price from 495 to 1,500 TWD; market cap recovered to ~NT$1.7T, making Yageo the 7th-largest TWSE name, surpassing Fubon Financial and UMC. Backdrop: Yageo signaled further price hikes (May 28) as Murata/SEMCO/Panasonic raise MLCC and capacitor prices on AI demand. Today's limit-up is part fundamental (record revenue, pricing power) and part flow (release from disposition restrictions plus a 3x analyst target hike triggering a sector-wide limit-up wave).

sources.label: NextApple (Jun 9, 2026): Yageo limit-up, record May revenue, Citi TP to 1,500
CTEE (Jun 9, 2026): Yageo exits disposition, limit-up; May revenue record; TP 1,500
Yahoo TW (Jun 9, 2026): 10k lots queued at limit; mkt cap NT$1.7T, 7th on TWSE
UDN (Jun 9, 2026): Yageo limit-up leads passive-component sector rally
DIGITIMES (May 28, 2026): Yageo signals possible price hikes as Japanese/Korean makers raise prices

verdicts.rankRationale: On a crowding-sensitive alpha list, today is the textbook anti-alpha setup: limit-up out of disposition with 10k lots queued, a Citi target tripled to 1,500, and a 243% YTD run making it the 7th-largest TWSE stock. The May revenue beat is real, but it is now fully consensus and over-owned, compressing residual alpha further from an already 'crowded/rerated benchmark' slot.
verdicts.bull: AI passives demand is accelerating, not peaking: record May revenue +47.5% YoY, sector-wide price hikes (Murata/SEMCO/Panasonic), and Yageo's own signaled increases give multi-quarter earnings momentum.
verdicts.neutral: Fundamentals and price are rising together; the stock is a fairly-priced sector benchmark rather than a hidden bottleneck, so it earns benchmark status but no alpha premium.
verdicts.bear: A limit-up driven partly by disposition-release flow and a headline-grabbing 3x target hike marks peak retail/institutional crowding; any MLCC pricing pause or AI-server order digestion triggers a sharp de-rating from 243% YTD highs.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Further MLCC/tantalum price-hike announcements and June monthly revenue confirming the +47% YoY run-rate.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Crowding unwind: post-limit-up flow exhaustion plus any sign of passive-component pricing or AI-server order moderation.

verdicts.rankRationale: On the broader board-level power-and-passives lens, the record May revenue (+47.5% YoY) and confirmed pricing power are exactly the execution evidence the thesis said to watch, validating the power-delivery content story. That genuine fundamental beat roughly offsets the additional crowding/rerating penalty from today's limit-up, so hold at rank after yesterday's down move.
verdicts.bull: Execution is verifiably strong: record monthly revenue, mid-teens AI revenue share and rising, tantalum/MLCC price hikes sticking, and Q2 margins guided above Q1's 38.1% gross margin.
verdicts.neutral: Thesis intact and now well-evidenced, but at ~$50B market cap and 7th-largest on TWSE the market fully recognizes the power-delivery content story; expect returns to track earnings, not rerating.
verdicts.bear: Passive components remain cyclical; the +47% YoY comp gets harder into 2H26, and a 1,500 TWD sell-side target sets a high bar where any execution wobble or MLCC price rollback hits a now-expensive multiple.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Q2 2026 results confirming gross/operating margin expansion above Q1 and continued AI revenue-share gains.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Cyclical pricing reversal in MLCC/tantalum once Japanese/Korean capacity responds, against a fully-rerated valuation.
